name: Conquest Planning
ai_strategy_summary: Conquest Planning serves 70% of Canadian financial advisors and 1.5M+ households, raised $80M Series B in June 2025, and launched four new AI-powered planning capabilities — making it the dominant AI financial planning platform in Canada with rapid US and UK expansion.
headline_metric: 70% of Canadian advisors; 1.5M+ households; $80M Series B June 2025
headline_initiative: AI-powered financial planning with scenario modeling and BMO partnership

advisor_productivity.headline: Four new AI-powered planning capabilities launched April 2025; advisors reduce planning time from hours to minutes
advisor_productivity.detail: Conquest launched four new AI-powered capabilities in April 2025 that enable advisors to build comprehensive financial plans in minutes — automating scenario modeling, goal-based planning frameworks, and plan generation. BMO partnership announced July 2025 lets BMO clients build their own financial plans via Conquest.
evidence: $80M Series B led by investors June 2025
Advisors reducing planning time from hours to minutes
advisor_productivity.jpm_implication: AI-powered financial planning that cuts plan generation from hours to minutes is becoming a standard advisor expectation. JPM's MoneyGuide/planning tools must match this speed and quality.

investment_portfolio.headline: AI scenario modeling and goal-based planning for 1.5M+ client households across 3 countries
investment_portfolio.detail: Conquest's core platform provides AI-powered scenario modeling — running multiple financial planning scenarios simultaneously to show clients the impact of different decisions — serving 1.5M+ households across Canada, US, and UK.
investment_portfolio.jpm_implication: Conquest's deep penetration of Canadian advisory (70% share) means it will follow Canadian advisors into US markets. JPM's US advisor planning tools must be competitive with Conquest's AI capabilities.

client_personalization.headline: Personalized AI financial plans delivered directly to 1.5M+ households; BMO client self-service launched July 2025
client_personalization.detail: Conquest's platform generates fully personalised financial plans for each of the 1.5M+ households it serves — tailoring goal-based scenarios to individual financial situations. The July 2025 BMO partnership extended this directly to banking clients, enabling BMO customers to build their own AI-powered financial plans without an advisor.
evidence: BMO partnership (July 2025) — bank clients build own plans via Conquest (Conquest Planning press release)
4 new AI capabilities launched April 2025 include personalised plan generation
client_personalization.jpm_implication: Conquest's B2B2C model — enabling banks to offer self-service AI planning to retail clients — threatens the entry-level advisory value proposition. JPM's self-directed and lower-tier CWM clients are a direct competitive battleground.

new_business_models.headline: B2B2C banking distribution model: BMO partnership lets bank clients self-serve AI financial plans
new_business_models.detail: The July 2025 BMO partnership represents a new distribution model for Conquest — embedding its AI financial planning engine inside a major bank's client-facing digital experience. This B2B2C model monetises Conquest's platform through bank licensing rather than direct advisor subscriptions, expanding the addressable market to retail banking clients who would not traditionally access an independent financial planner.
evidence: BMO partnership announced July 2025 — B2B2C distribution confirmed
$80M Series B (June 2025) explicitly to fund US and UK expansion
Goldman Sachs Alternatives led the Series B — signalling institutional confidence in the model
new_business_models.jpm_implication: If banks begin embedding third-party AI planning tools into their retail digital experience, the role of captive bank advisors in delivering financial plans diminishes. JPM must determine whether to build, buy, or partner to offer comparable self-serve planning capability.
